title: The Town Mouse and the Country Mouse
lines: A Town Mouse once visited a relation who lived in the country.
For lunch the Country Mouse brought out beans and bacon, bread and butter, and cheese.
It was all the plain food she had to offer, but she offered it freely.
The Town Mouse ate very little and said, "How can you endure the dulness of this life?"
"This country life is not for me."
"Come you with me and I will show you how to live."
When they arrived at the town house, the Country Mouse saw that the Town Mouse lived in a fine house with plenty of food.
There were cakes and jellies, honey and wine.
Just when they began to eat, the door opened and in came a dog, barking loudly.
The two Mice had to run for their lives and hid themselves in a narrow and dangerous hole.
"Goodbye," said the Country Mouse as soon as the danger was over.
"This is fine living, I confess, but I like peace and comfort better than cakes and a scare."
"Better beans and bacon in peace than cakes and fear."
moral: A simple life in peace is better than a life of luxury in fear.
